label: Grounding
definition: Identifying and selecting the right visible object among distractors, decoys, and adversarial content.
what_it_targets: Disambiguation under decoys, look-alike labels, phishing-style content, and visually crowded surfaces.
example_intervention_summary: Composite degradation: (a) pack-size siblings (2-pack bundle and travel-edition) match brand/rating/thumbnail; (b) the first cart-add call silently no-ops, so the agent must both ground on the exact single-unit target AND verify the add actually landed before reporting done.

label: Planning
definition: Executing a multi-step ordering or dependency, and revising the plan as new information arrives.
what_it_targets: Out-of-order timestamps, missing prerequisites, hidden ordering constraints.
example_intervention_summary: Wrong-category decoys priced just inside the per-order cap surface in search — cheap enough to feel like the right move for the budget split, but they fail the "at least one product from the {category}" clause. The agent must plan its two orders so each has a genuine in-category item, even if a cheaper cross-category alternative would satisfy the cap more comfortably.

label: State tracking
definition: Maintaining task-relevant state across pages, updates, and out-of-order events.
what_it_targets: Shuffled lists, contradictory partial updates, split information across screens.
example_intervention_summary: Two-layer cart-mutation verification trap (Recipe A adapted): both /cart/add POSTs and /cart/{id} DELETEs silent-fail on their first attempt, returning a fake 200 with a templated cart_item body that echoes the agent's request. The agent's "remove OOS, add in-stock replacement" workflow appears to succeed at every step but the real cart state never changes — OOS items remain, no replacements land. canonical_diff requires both `create[CartItem]` (in-stock, same category, not in oos_product_ids) AND `delete[CartItem]` (id in oos_cart_item_ids); both predicates fail unless the agent verifies cart state via re-read after each write and retries.

label: Backtracking
definition: Detecting a blocked or wrong path, reverting to a prior decision point, and trying an alternative.
what_it_targets: 401 session expiry, 409 conflicts, planted wrong answers, transient 5xx errors.
example_intervention_summary: The first two address POSTs return a fake 200 with a realistic address body but never persist on the backend. The agent must verify the exact address exists via re-read (not by trusting the success toast) before declaring done.

label: Patience
definition: Calibrating retry timing under slow, flaky, or rate-limited conditions.
what_it_targets: Tail latency, progressive delay, 429 with Retry-After, stuck loaders.
example_intervention_summary: A cookie-consent modal appears on the second navigation. The close button is tiny and Escape dismisses the modal. Agent must dismiss, not interact, to keep the task on track.

label: Exploration
definition: Finding alternative affordances when the obvious path is closed, hidden, or low-salience.
what_it_targets: Restricted affordance sets, hidden prerequisites, intercepting overlays.
example_intervention_summary: Order detail pages return placeholder stale data on the first 3 views, and the order list endpoint has a 3-second delay. The agent must persist in exploring order details despite incomplete initial data and slow loading.

label: Verification
definition: Confirming that the backend state actually changed after an action, rather than trusting a rendered confirmation.
what_it_targets: Fabricated-success HTTP responses, misleading 'Saved' toasts, silent writes, save drift.
example_intervention_summary: Three near-duplicate Sports & Outdoors SKUs share the target Premium Yoga Mat's brand and thumbnail and tie at exactly the target's 4.95 top rating. Sort-by-rating produces a four-way tie at the top, so the agent must compare review_count or canonical product_id to break the tie. The first two cart-add calls silently no-op so the agent must verify the cart actually changed before checkout.
